title: Cross-cutting — ISE Admin Session Anomaly Evidence (Geographic, Concurrent)
exclusions: Approved travel admin accounts excluded via lookup.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 8.2.7 — admin session controls, including impossible-travel detection.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Admin login from two countries within 5 min. Evidence captured.

dataSources: `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` admin events + GeoIP. Cross-reference UC-17.1.72.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263) + Splunk Add-on for Cisco Identity Services (1915).
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode=60005 earliest=-1d
| iplocation source_ip
| stats dc(source_ip) as ips, values(Country) as countries by admin_user
| where ips > 2 OR mvcount(countries) > 1
| eval evidence_status="admin_session_anomaly"
| collect index=audit_evidence
description: We continuously evidence admin session anomalies (impossible-travel, multi-IP) for cross-cutting privileged-access compliance.
value: Multiple frameworks require monitoring of privileged-account anomalies. This UC produces the cross-framework evidence.
implementation: Schedule the saved search daily at 06:00 local time; archive results to the `audit_evidence` summary index for the cross-cutting MFA-control evidence pack.

references.title: UC-17.1.72 — ISE GUI Admin Session Activity

detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- UC-17.1.72 operational; GeoIP database installed.

## Step 1-5

```ini
[UC-22.40.7: Admin Session Anomaly Coverage]
cron_schedule = 0 7 * * *
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.40.7,family=privileged_access
```
grandmaExplanation: We continuously evidence admin session anomalies for cross-cutting privileged-access compliance. That is important for the audit, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.
